column13: To provide a machine learning device and a machine learning method for reliably selecting an appropriate explanatory variable from among specific target variables and a plurality of variable candidates in a learning model.SOLUTION: A machine learning device 200 includes: a plurality of sparse modeling parts 213 which use mutually different sparse modeling techniques to acquire respective individual degrees of significance as explanatory variables for explaining specific target variables for a respective plurality of explanatory variable candidates where the specific target variables and the plurality of explanatory variable candidates in a machine model used for industrial activities are provided as input data of the sparse modeling technique; and a selection part 215 which calculates an overall degree of significance for each of the explanatory variable candidates based on the plurality of individual degrees of significance for the respective explanatory variable candidates and selects the explanatory variable of the learning model from among the plurality of explanatory variable candidates based on the overall degree of significance.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 4

column12: MACHINE LEARNING APPARATUS AND MACHINE LEARNING PROGRAM
column13: To provide a machine learning apparatus enabling high-accuracy character recognition without a need for an expensive library with a huge number of data.SOLUTION: A machine learning apparatus includes an imaging unit 31 that images a handwritten character, a generation unit 32 that generates first character information by performing character recognition to a handwritten image of the imaged handwritten character by the imaging unit, and a learning unit 33 that acquires correctness information indicating correctness of the first character information and performs learning using the image data of the handwritten image, the first character information, and the correctness information as teacher data.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 3

column12: MACHINE LEARNING SYSTEM AND MACHINE LEARNING METHOD
column13: To provide a machine learning system and a machine learning method that perform machine learning.SOLUTION: A machine learning system includes a plurality of information processing devices communicably connected, and performs machine learning by a parallel distributed system using the plurality of information processing devices. The information processing device performs transmission and reception of parameters used for model learning in machine learning with other information processing device, and when parameters are transmitted to other information processing devices, the parameters are differentially coded and compressed, and then, the parameters received from the other information processing devices are decompressed. The information processing device performs differential encoding by respectively calculating a difference in parameters between adjacent positions at a same time in a model space of a model (for example, a convolution neural network). Further, the information processing device performs differential encoding by respectively calculating a difference in parameters between successive times at a same position in a model space of a model (for example, feedforward neural network).SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 4

column12: MACHINE-LEARNING DEVICE AND MACHINE-LEARNING SYSTEM
column13: The purpose of the present invention is to provide a machine-learning device and a machine-learning system which can reduce the size of learning data and perform learning fast. The machine-learning device comprises: a machine-learning unit which learns learning data including images and labels for the images; an image processing unit which processes the images by using an image processing program; a small-size learning data creation unit which cuts off, from the image, a partial image to be used for learning by the machine-learning unit, and creates small-size learning data including the partial image; and a learning data control unit which stores the small-size learning data in association with the image processing program, wherein the machine-learning unit learns the learning data or the small-size learning data.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING DEVICE AND MACHINE LEARNING METHOD
column13: An activation state decision unit includes a plurality of parameter units made to process data on the basis of parameters respectively managed thereby. Each of the parameter units includes a number generator that generates a numerical number a sign of which varies, a number processor (such as an adder) that creates a parameter to process the data on the basis of the parameter and the numerical number generated by the number generator, and a parameter updating unit that updates the parameter on the basis of a cost value, which is acquired by evaluation of the processed data by an evaluation system, and the numerical number generated by the number generator. The number generator changes the generated numerical number in each data processing, and generates the numerical number in such a manner that order of a sign variation of the numerical number varies between the parameter units.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING METHOD AND MACHINE LEARNING SYSTEM
column13: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a machine learning method and a machine learning system with which a search engine can display highly relevant web pages on the top of a list of search results even with limited learning data.SOLUTION: In order to obtain a ranking function for a search engine 15, firstly, a learning model is obtained through learning based on the importance of learning data examples. Secondly, the search system ranks web pages using the learning model and then, the ranking result is compared with correct answer data, a collection of web pages relevant to a search term, stored in a storage means in advance, and an evaluation value for the search system is computed based on the comparison result.

column12: Machine learning method and machine learning system
column13: The invention relates to a machine learning method and a machine learning system to suppress excessive increase of processing load of a server. A machine learning method for machine learning a value of a model parameter, which is performed in a machine learning system (1) provided with a vehicle (2) having a machine learning model and a server (3) capable of communicating with the vehicle, comprises the steps of: detecting a current processing load of the server by the server; the server determines a processing amount of machine learning performed in each of the server and the vehicle on the basis of the processing load; the server performs machine learning on the value of the model parameter according to the determined processing amount of machine learning in the server; and the vehicle performs machine learning on the value of the model parameter according to the determined processing amount of machine learning in the vehicle. The server reduces the processing amount of machine learning in the server when the processing load is relatively high than when the processing load is relatively low.

column12: Machine learning device and machine learning program
column13: Provided are a machine learning device and a machine learning program that are capable of improving the accuracy of individual identification, emotion recognition, and physical condition estimation. A face feature amount calculation unit (31) calculates a face feature amount from a face image, and a face individual identification unit (42) compares the calculated face feature amount with a face feature amount for individual identification in a label DB (56). When a difference between the face feature amount calculated by the face feature amount calculation unit (31) and the face feature amount for individual identification in the label DB (56) is larger than a prescribed value, the face feature amount in the label DB (56) is updated by the face feature amount calculated by the face feature amount calculation unit (31). When the face feature amount calculation unit (31) calculates a face feature amount that remains similar for a prescribed time and the label DB (56) contains no record of emotion of an identified individual including a feature amount similar to said face feature amount, the record of said individual including emotion resulting from a query and said face feature amount is added to the label DB (56).

column13: To generate a learning model with a high correct answer rate and with high accuracy.SOLUTION: A machine learning device is composed of a computer including an arithmetic device which executes prescribed arithmetic processing and a storage device connected to the arithmetic device. The arithmetic device comprises an importance discrimination unit which selects a parameter input to a machine learning model as an explanatory variable on the basis of a correlationship between parameters, and a model creation unit in which the arithmetic device creates a machine learning model.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 3

column12: MACHINE LEARNING METHOD AND MACHINE LEARNING DEVICE
column13: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a method for enabling the learning rate to be autonomously optimized in the learning process such as a neural network and preventing learning from being led to a dead end.SOLUTION: A storage device holds programs, a plurality of systems including a plurality of structure parameters corresponding to the programs, and a learning parameter corresponding to each system. A machine learning method includes: the first procedure for causing each system to learn a prescribed data set by using a learning parameter corresponding to each system; the second procedure for evaluating each system by a prescribed evaluation method; and the third procedure for selecting the first system having the lower evaluation and the second system having the higher evaluation than the first system out of the plurality of systems, generating the copy of the second system and changing at least one of the learning parameter corresponding to the second system and the learning parameter corresponding to the copy of the second system. The machine learning method executes the first procedure to the third procedure again for the plurality of systems other than the first system.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 5

column12: MACHINE LEARNING METHOD AND MACHINE LEARNING APPARATUS
column13: A machine learning method includes acquiring teacher data to be used in supervised learning, and plurality of document data, specifying first document data among the plurality of document data in accordance with a first feature value and a second feature value, the first feature value being decided in accordance with a frequency of appearance of a word in the teacher data, the second feature value being decided in accordance with a frequency of appearance of the word in each of the plurality of document data, and performing machine-learning of characteristic information of the first document data as pre-learning for the supervised learning.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING METHOD AND MACHINE LEARNING SYSTEM
column13: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a machine learning method and a machine learning system with which even when each learning data generated from each of information sources to be integrated has an unique type of feature quantity, a ranking function for integration can be obtained using the unique type of feature quantity as it is.SOLUTION: In order to obtain a ranking function of a retrieval system, firstly, the importance and the class of an example containing a same search term are calculated as relative values obtained for the example in comparison with all other examples in a collection of examples, the collection of examples consisting of examples containing the same search term, obtained out of an integrated learning data obtained by integrating each of learning data generated from each of multiple information sources. Secondly, a weak hypothesis is generated for each of the multiple information sources by performing machine learning using the importance and the class, and then, the importance of the weak hypothesis, which is common for all the weak hypothesis generated, is computed. Finally, a ranking function for each of the multiple information sources is updated based on the generated weak hypothesis and the computed importance of weak hypothesis.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING METHOD AND MACHINE LEARNING DEVICE
column13: A machine learning method and a machine learning device are provided. The machine learning method includes: receiving an input signal and performing normalization on the input signal; transmitting the normalized input signal to a convolutional layer; and adding a sparse coding layer after the convolutional layer, wherein the sparse coding layer uses dictionary atoms to reconstruct signals on a projection of the normalized input signal passing through the convolutional layer, and the sparse coding layer receives a mini-batch input to refresh the dictionary atoms.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING DEVICE AND MACHINE LEARNING METHOD
column13: Good performance is obtained even with a small amount of training data, by reducing time and labor required for collecting training data for use in training. This machine learning device has an acquisition unit that acquires inference data and training data for use in machine learning, a training unit that performs machine learning based on the training data and sets of training parameters and generates trained models, a model assessment unit assesses whether or not the trained results by the trained models are good and displays the assessment results, a model selection unit that can receive a selected trained model, an inference calculation unit that performs inference calculation processing based on at least a part of the trained models and the inference data and generates inference result candidates, and an inference determination unit that outputs at least part of the inference result candidates or the combinations thereof.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING METHOD, AND MACHINE LEARNING DEVICE
column13: A machine-learning method includes: a data-set division process of separating a period into a learning period and a validation period, and dividing data set into learning data and validation data; a learning-candidate-period separating process of separating learning candidate period into a plurality of divided learning periods; a learning-period evaluation process of generating a learning-period evaluation model for each divided learning period using learning data corresponding to the divided learning periods, and calculating an evaluation index of the learning-period evaluation model for each divided learning period; a learning-period selection process of repeatedly performing the learning-candidate-period separating process and the learning-period evaluation process until a predetermined termination condition is satisfied, and selecting a divided learning period having a high evaluation index as a learning target period when the termination condition is satisfied; and a model output process of outputting the learning model on which machine learning has been performed using the learning data corresponding to the learning target period.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING PROGRAM AND MACHINE LEARNING METHOD
column13: To improve inference accuracy of machine learning models.SOLUTION: For a machine learning model having a plurality of layers that are subjected to learning in advance, a first output layer formed in conformity with a downstream task and connected to the last layer of a plurality of layers, and a plurality of second output layers connected to respective outputs of layers other than the last layer among the plurality of layers and having the same configuration as the first output layer, after training only the first output layer and the second output layers of the machine learning model with the downstream task, the entire machine learning model including the first output layer and the second output layers is trained with the downstream task.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 5

column12: LEARNING MACHINE
column13: PURPOSE:To complete a learning operation in the small weight changing frequency by increasing the learning rate and the weight changed variable through a straight line searching part if the output signal of an output layer has no change despite the weight change and then changing the weight. CONSTITUTION:The multi-input/output circuits 94, 95 and 96 apply the weight to the input signals and add together these weighted signals. Then the non-linear processing is applied to the input signal according to the characteristic function of each adder for acquisition of an output signal. A learning circuit 93 changes the weights multiplied by the variable weight multipliers 3-8 so that the coincidence is secured between the output of an output layer 21 and the output of a teacher signal generating part 13. An error calculation part 14 outputs an error signal between an actual output signal and a teacher signal. A weight changing direction deciding part 15 calculates the most suddenly falling direction (g) based on the output and input signals of a hidden layer 22, the output signal of the layer 21, and the weight of the layer 21. The direction (g) is outputted to a straight line searching part 67. Thus the weight is changed so as to change the output of the layer 21 even when the output signal of the multi-input/output circuit is shown in the limited accuracy. Then a learning operation is completed in the small weight changing frequency.

column12: Machine learning
column13: Computer implemented machine learning methods are described. A co-operative learning method involves a first rule based system and a second rule based system. A rule base is generated from input data and recursion data is used to recursively update the rule base as a result of newly received input data. Rule data defining at least one rule and associated data are sent to the second system which determines whether to update its rule base using the transmitted rule data, and if so the recursion data is used to recursively determine the updated rules for its rule base. A father machine learning method for a rule based system, involves receiving time series data, determining whether the data increases or decreases the spatial density for previously existing rules, and if so then creating a new cluster and associated rule, otherwise a new cluster is not created.

column12: LEARNING MACHINE
column13: PURPOSE:To reproduce a required part in a short time at learner's request by converting currently reproduced analog sound signals into digital signals and storing them according to a recording specifying signal from a teaching material, and inhibiting analog sound signals that are digitally recorded from being reproduced as sound. CONSTITUTION:This teaching machine is provided with a means 6 which converts currently reproduced analog sound signals into digital signals and stores them according to a recording specifying signal from the teaching mate rial 4. Moreover, the machine is provided with a switch means 7 which prevents the learner from listening to analog sound signals that are currently recorded. While the user cannot listen to a specific part during the reproduction of the teaching material 4, the analog sound signals in the specific part are converted into digital signals to be in the specific part are converted into digital signals to be stored. Thus, drills and answers can be digitally recorded without letting the learner know them.

column12: Learning machine
column13: A learning machine belongs to stationery commodities, mainly solves the problems that the traditional learning machine is inconvenient to carry, and a matched stylus pen is easy to lose, and comprises a learning machine body, wherein a hanging ring is arranged at the corner of the top end of the learning machine body; one end of a connecting rope is connected on the hanging ring, and the other end of the connecting rope is connected with the stylus pen; one end of the stylus pen is provided with a pen point, and the other end of the stylus pen is provided with a thread; one side of the learning machine body is provided with a cylindrical groove, and the top end of a cylindrical groove is provided with an inner thread matched with the outer thread. The stylus pen is connected on the learning machine through the connecting rope, and can be conveniently placed when being used without worrying losing of the stylus pen; when being not used, the pen can be placed in the cylindrical groove and screwed, and the connecting rope can be used as a handle under the self-lock function in matching of the threads, thus facilitating the carrying of the learning machine; and the learning machine body can be hung on the wall through the connecting rope when being placed, thus being convenient to place.

column12: Machine learning

column13: The utility model discloses a learning machine. The learning machine comprises a housing, a human body motion amplitude detection device, a main control board and a mobile terminal. Wherein the shell is provided with a forward panel used for facing a user, the human motion amplitude detection device is installed on the shell and is exposed from the forward panel, the main control board is installed in the shell and is electrically connected with the human motion amplitude detection device, and the mobile terminal is in communication connection with the main control board. According to the scheme, the behavior of the user is detected through the human body motion amplitude detection device, and the main control board can judge whether the user is in a normal learning state or not according to the detection result of the human body motion amplitude detection device; and if the user does frequent movement or large-amplitude movement within a certain time or the face and the sight are not within a preset range for a long time, the abnormal behavior can be recorded and transmitted to the mobile terminal in the hand of the parent, so that the user is supervised.

column12: LEARNING MACHINE
column13: PURPOSE:To shorten the time required for learning by determining the steepest drop direction as a minimum error point search direction when a last weight variation quantity is less than a constant threshold value and determining a conjugate gradient direction in other cases. CONSTITUTION:In a 1st minimum error point search, a search direction determining means 16 determines the steepest drop direction as the minimum error search direction in a weight space represented by weight vectors of variable weight multiplying means 3 - 8. In 2nd and succeeding minimum error searches, the search direction determining means 16 determines the steepest drop direction as the minimum error point search direction in the weight space when the last weight variation quantity is less than the constant threshold value or the conjugate gradient direction in other cases. Thus, the best minimum error point search direction is found to make the error sufficiently small in a short learning time and the learning is completed.
column15: SAKAGAMI SHIGEO;;KODA TOSHIYUKI;;SHIMEKI TAIJI;;TAKAGI HIDEYUKI;;TOGAWA HAYATO

column12: LEARNING MACHINE
column13: PURPOSE:To shorten a learning time by adjusting weight at the time of detecting a non-efficient state in which the error of a learning state is not reduced even if the learning is advanced furthermore, and then restarting the learning. CONSTITUTION:Non-linear processing is applied to the weighted sum of input signals by means of a characteristic function having saturated characteristics and the processed result is outputted from a hidden layer 22 and the non-linear processing is applied to the weighted sum of the output signals from the hidden layer 22 and the processed result is outputted from an output layer 21. A teacher signal generating part 13 generates a required output signal for the output layer 21 and conjugate inclination direction determining part 15 uses the calculation value of an error calculating part 14 and determines a weight changing direction so that an error between the outputs of the generation part 13 and the output layer 21 is minimized. When an improper learning state detecting part 92 detects a non-efficient state in which the error is not reduced even when the learning is advanced furthermore, the determining part 65 adjusts the weight. When the learning is restarted after adjusting the weight, a proper state is obtained and the learning time is shortened.

column12: LEARNING MACHINE
column13: PURPOSE:To provide a learning machine which can perform the learning at a high speed and with high efficiency and also can perform the learning additional to the unknown input date at 8 high speed with the influence of recognizing performance minimized to the learned data. CONSTITUTION:A tree branch structure consists of a combination of the unit recognizing units n11-n12, n21-n30, p31-p350 and q41-q43. Then 8 series of various feature date on e subject are inputted to the signal input part of the unit recognizing unit of each hierarchy of each tree branch structure. Each of these units has a quentizer which carries out the quantization in accordance with the input signal and switches the coupling routes of the unit recognizing units of lower orders of a network based on the output of the quentizer to decide an election route covering down to the unit of the lowest order. Then the learning is performed when the coupling intensity is changed by a learning device between the route input terminals of the unit recognizing units (p31-p350) of orders preceding the lowest order and the route output terminal pointed by the output value of the quantizer.

column12: LEARNING MACHINE
column13: PURPOSE:To minimize an error with high efficiency and to shorten the learning time by obtaining the optimum value of a learning parameter in the minimum point searching direction of the error and at the same time changing the weight of a variable weight multiplier. CONSTITUTION:A learning parameter near the minimum point of an error set in its searching direction is extracted 18 after decision 16 of the searching direction of a point where the error is minimized in a weight space. The paraboloidal approximation is applied to the periphery of the minimum point of the error and an error produced at an apex of the paraboloid is extracted 19. Then the weight of each of variable weight multipliers 3 - 8 is changed 20 by means of a learning parameter obtained with the minimum error. Then the searching direction is obtained for a new minimum error point and the optimum value of the learning parameter obtained in the preceding search of the minimum point is reflected to set 17 the initial value of the learning parameter. The minimum error point between both searching direction is obtained and the weight is changed. An error minimizing circuit 21 of such a constitution searches continuously the minimum error points until the error is reduced down to a satisfactory level.

column13: Computer implemented machine learning methods are described. A co-operative learning method involves a first rule based system and a second rule based system. A rule base is generated from input data and recursion data is used to recursively update the rule base as a result of newly received input data. Rule data defining at least one rule and associated data are sent to the second system which determines whether to update its rule base using the transmitted rule data, and if so the recursion data is used to recursively determine the updated rules for its rule base. A father machine learning method for a rule based system, involves receiving time series data, determining whether the data increases or decreases the spatial density for previously existing rules, and if so then creating a new cluster and associated rule, otherwise a new cluster is not created.

column13: An automated response system (e.g., an automated voice response system) may employ learning strategies to develop or improve automated response capabilities. Learning strategies may include using communications(e.g., utterances, text messages, etc.) of one party in a conversation (e.g., a customer service agent) to identify and categorize communications of another party in the conversation (e.g., a caller). Classifiers can be build from the categorized communications. Classifiers can be used to identify common communications patterns of a party in a conversation (e.g., an agent). column13: Computer implemented machine learning methods are described. A co-operative learning method involves a first rule based system and a second rule based system. A rule base is generated from input data and recursion data is used to recursively update the rule base as a result of newly received input data. Rule data defining at least one rule and associated data are sent to the second system which determines whether to update its rule base using the transmitted rule data, and if so the recursion data is used to recursively determine the updated rules for its rule base. A father machine learning method for a rule based system, involves receiving time series data, determining whether the data increases or decreases the spatial density for previously existing rules, and if so then creating a new cluster and associated rule, otherwise a new cluster is not created.

column13: A computer-implemented method, a machine learning system, and non-transitory computer-readable storage medium for training a neural network are provided. The neural network is used to instruct an agent to select actions for interacting with an environment to determine a solution to a specified problem. In the computer-implemented method a state signal representing a current state of the environment is received. A Sequential Monte Carlo process is then used to perform a search to determine target action selection data associated with the current state of the environment. This target action selection data is stored in association with the state signal and the current state of the environment is updated by providing an action selection signal based on the target action selection data. The Sequential Monte Carlo process involves generating a plurality of simulations using the neural network to determine the target action selection data.

column13: PURPOSE:To end the learning with continuous change of the weight even when the weight or the weight changed variable are shown in the limited accuracy by changing the leaning rate based on the error between the weighting output signal of the signal processing obtained when the learning is carried out by a learning machine and a desirable output signal. CONSTITUTION:A learning machine consists of the multi-input/output circuits 94-96 containing the variable weight multipliers 3-8 and the adders 9-11 having the saturated input/output characteristics connected together in a hierarchical form. The circuits 94-96 adds together the input signals with weighting and then apply the non-linear processing to the input signals in accordance with the characteristic function of each adder to obtain the output signals. A learning circuit 93 changes the weights that are multiplied by the multipliers 3-8 so that the output of an output layer 21 is equal to the output of a teacher signal generating part 13. The part 13 outputs a desirable output signal, i.e., a teacher signal to an error calculation part 14. The part 14 outputs a difference signal between the actual output signal and the teacher signal to a weight changing direction deciding part 15. Thus the part 15 obtains the most suddenly falling direction ' g' from the output and input signals of a hidden layer 22, the output signal of the layer 21, and the weight of the layer 21 and then outputs the direction ' g' to a straight line searching part 67.

column12: LEARNING MACHINE
column13: PURPOSE:To improve the efficiency of learning by providing an error signal decision part and a weight variation quantity control part and inhibiting weight variation from being performed by a multi-input/output signal processing part which is sufficiently small in error extent. CONSTITUTION:A signal is inputted to the input part 4 of an output signal calculation part 1, a multi-input/output signal processing part 3 multiplies the output of a multi- input/output signal processing part in a lower layer by a weight coefficient (connection extent) stored in a memory 6 through a multiplier 7 and finds the sum of products by an adder 8, and a threshold value processing part 9 converts it to calculate an input value to an input part 5. A weight coefficient updating part 2 generates a tutor signal 10 corresponding to the signal of an output signal calculation part 1 and an error signal calculation part 11 finds the error from the actual output signal of the calculation part 1 to evaluate the performance of a circuit network in a current coupling state (weight coefficient value). A weight variation quantity calculation part 12 finds the variation quantity of the weight coefficient in the memory 6 according to the calculated error and a control part 13 compares it with a threshold value by a decision part 14 and updates the weight repeatedly to set the weight coefficient variation quantity to '0' when the error becomes small enough, thereby finishing the learning operation.

column13: 1,017,841. Automatic learning circuits. ANDROMEDA Inc. Feb. 6, 1962, No. 4610/62. Heading G4R. In a learning circuit, i.e. a circuit which will produce a predetermined response to each of a series of input signal patterns, the input patterns are applied each as an ordered group of n separate values and is interacted in the circuit with a stored memory function (also containing an ordered group of n separate values) the circuit serving to form products of corresponding applied and stored values which are summed to develop a classifying signal. This signal has one of a number of predetermined values which is converted by a register unit to a standing output identifying the input pattern. The input signal patterns may be a series of instantaneous sample values of the signal derived from speaking a word into a microphone or from scanning a character. The series of sample values are treated as a multidimensional vector signal having n compartments. The apparatus may be designed to learn and recognize a family of vectors by suitable progressive adjustment of the stored vector G. A particular input vector is termed F and the components are Fi. The classifying signal referred to above is the sealer product of the two vectors F and G. This is the sum of each component of the vector F multiplied by the corresponding component of the stored vector G. In the learning operation the vector F to be learned is applied together with an assigned sealer value B. The learning process is iterative, i.e. the stored vector G gradually assumes a value which will cause the proper output V to appear when the vector F is applied, V being equal to B, the assigned sealer value. During learning error values are produced which are the differences between the output produced and the assigned output value. From the error signal correction signals are obtained to be added to the stored values to produce a new stored vector value. This process, which is repeated for all the input vectors to be learned is the equivalent of solving a set of simultaneous linear equations by iteration. In the diagrammatic form of Fig. 3 the stored values are signals G 1 (x) &c. in conditioned memory 302. Sequence control 306 controls switches 303, 304, 311 and 318. Switch 304 passes the correction signals from generator 305 to the memory to modify the stored signals. Switch 303 connects the stored signals to function operator 308 where they are processed in turn with the incoming vector components F 1 (x) to be learned the products being summed to produce an output signal on line 320. The desired output signal is supplied from unit 310, selected in turn by switch 311 and applied on lead 313 to error detector 314 which subtracts the actual and desired outputs to produce the error signal to be applied to generator 305. This also receives the input vector component and produces the correction values to modify the stored signals. The transformation unit includes means to normalize for amplitude and for duration. For the latter the incoming waveforms are stored at one speed and re-read at a different speed to produce a signal of constant duration. In the recognition phase the output of the function operation 308, having a predetermined value indicative of the corresponding learned waveform, is applied to comparator 308. If the output signal is equal to any of the specified ranges a corresponding digitalized response is produced on output leads 319.

column13: An automated response system (e.g., an automated voice response system) may employ learning strategies to develop or improve automated response capabilities. Learning strategies may include using communications(e.g., utterances, text messages, etc.) of one party in a conversation (e.g., a customer service agent) to identify and categorize communications of another party in the conversation (e.g., a caller). Classifiers can be build from the categorized communications. Classifiers can be used to identify common communications patterns of a party in a conversation (e.g., an agent). column13: 1,255,614. Learning machines. MATSUSHITA ELECTRIC INDUSTRIAL CO. Ltd. 11 Dec., 1968 [18 Dec., 1967; 15 Oct., 1968; 20 Nov., 1968], No. 69023/68. Heading G4R. A learning machine comprises a plurality of weight elements respectively associated with a plurality of input terminals, decision means for comparing the sum of the outputs from the weight elements with a threshold value, means for delivering an error signal representing error between the output signal of said decision means and a predetermined desired output, a learning pulse generator for generating at least one learning pulse in response to said error signal, and control switch circuit means for controlling the supply of the learning pulse to the weight elements to vary the weights of the weight elements electronically until the error signal is reduced to zero. In a first embodiment, the control switch circuit means supplies each learning pulse to each weight element associated with an input terminal carrying a 1 signal, to increment a ring counter in the weight element, the ring counter enabling one of a plurality of transistors to pass a respective weighting voltage to a junction, from which it is passed to the summing circuit by a further transistor enabled by a 1 at the input terminal. The learning pulses are also counted to adjust the threshold if the error signal is not reduced to zero after a certain number of learning pulses. In another embodiment, the error signal, if non-zero, can have either polarity. As long as the error signal is non-zero, pulses are produced, each of which increments a ring counter which selects one of the inputs at a time. If the input signal is 1, a learning pulse is generated in response to this pulse and routed under control of the ring counter to the corresponding weight element which is adjusted up or down according to the polarity of the error signal (supplied to the learning pulse generator. The threshold is controlled by a multistable circuit driven by an AND gate enabled when all the input signals to the learning machine are 1 and any of the pulses produced in response to a non-zero error signal are present. A third embodiment supplies one or four learning pulses at a time (to increase or decrease the weights respectively), when the decision means output and desired output are unequal, depending on which is 1 and which 0. A fourth embodiment is a minor variation of the first.

column12: LEARNING MACHINE
column13: PURPOSE:To improve the efficiency and the accuracy of learning by constituting the learning machine so that a weight change in a multi-input-output signal processing part in which magnitude of an error is below a threshold is not executed, and also, the threshold becomes smaller as learning advances. CONSTITUTION:In an error signal calculating part 11, an error in the uppermost layer is calculated from a difference of an actual output signal outputted from an output signal calculating part 1 and a teacher signal, and based on the calculated error, a weight change amount calculating part 12 calculates a change amount of a weight coefficient stored in a memory of the output signal calculating part 1. Subsequently, when it is decided that the error is smaller than a set threshold T, a weight change amount control part 13 sets the weight coefficient change amount to '0'. In this case, a threshold control part 16 switches the threshold T to a smaller value as learning advances, based on a result of decision by a learning progress deciding part 15. In such a way, the learning efficiency and the accuracy are improved.

column13: 1317745 Learning machines MATSUSHITA ELECTRIC INDUSTRIAL CO Ltd 13 July 1970 [14 July 1969 (2)] 33958/70 Heading G4R A learning machine uses a plurality of items of standard pattern information from a memory in turn for training, weights being adjusted until a thresholded sum equals a desired value, when the next standard pattern is taken from the memory for use in the training. Prior to training, standard patterns are passed via an input buffer to a shift register memory which also receives the corresponding desired outputs. In training, each pattern from the memory in turn is passed into the input buffer, a weighted sum of the bits which are equal to 1 being thresholded at one of two thresholds depending on the desired output. If the results of this (the actual output) is equal to the desired output, the next pattern is obtained from the memory, but otherwise the weights which contributed are all incremented or all decremented as appropriate. During recognition, an input pattern in the input buffer forms a weighted sum which is thresholded at a constant level to give the output.

Learning strategies may include using communications(e.g., utterances, text messages, etc.) of one party in a conversation (e.g., a customer service agent) to identify and categorize communications of another party in the conversation (e.g., a caller). Classifiers can be build from the categorized communications. Classifiers can be used to identify common communications patterns of a party in a conversation (e.g., an agent). Learning strategies may also include selecting communications as learning opportunities to improve automated response capabilities based on selection criteria (e.g., selection criteria chosen to ensure that the system does not learn from unreliable or insignificant examples).

column13: Computer implemented machine learning methods are described. A co-operative learning method involves a first rule based system and a second rule based system. A rule base is generated from input data and recursion data is used to recursively update the rule base as a result of newly received input data. Rule data defining at least one rule and associated data are sent to the second system which determines whether to update its rule base using the transmitted rule data, and if so the recursion data is used to recursively determine the updated rules for its rule base. A father machine learning method for a rule based system, involves receiving time series data, determining whether the data increases or decreases the spatial density for previously existing rules, and if so then creating a new cluster and associated rule, otherwise a new cluster is not created.

column13: PURPOSE:To prevent the deterioration of learning speed and performance after learning when a bit width is narrow by deciding the change direction of the weight in a weight change part so as to minimize the error between the output signal of a teacher signal generation part and the one of an output layer and changing each weight while multiplying a learning rate in the direction of the change of the weight. CONSTITUTION:A multi-input/output circuit constituting a hidden layer and the output layer and a weight change pat changing the weighting in the multi- input/output circuit constitutes the learning machine equipped with a floating- point calculation part calculating by means of an exponent part composed of a (s) bit indicating the value which is more than Po and less than Po+2<s> (Po is an integral number less than -2s-1; the (s) is a natural number) and a mantissa pat composed of a (k) bit (the (k) is a natural number). The floating- point calculation is performed while using the exponent part and the mantissa part. Thus, the calculation with higher accuracy can be performed with the same bit width, and the learning machine can be constructed without deteriorat ing the speed of learning and the performance after learning even when the bit width of a variable is narrow.

column12: LEARNING MACHINE
column13: PURPOSE:To converge more quickly an output signal having a large error and to shorten the learning time by controlling the changed variable of a weight coefficient in response to the value of an error signal. CONSTITUTION:When a signal is inputted to an output signal calculation part 1, each multi-input/output signal processing part multiplies the output of the multi-input/output signal processing part of a connected lower layer by a weight coefficient of connection stored in a memory. The total sum of the multiplication outputs is added and turned into the threshold value. This value is outputted to the multi-input/output signal processing part of a higher layer. A weight coefficient updating part 2 produces a desirable output signal as a teacher signal in accordance with the input signal of the part 1. Then the part 2 calculates the error of the k-th multi-input/output signal processing part of the highest layer based on the difference between the actual output signal and the teacher signal and evaluates the network performance in the present connection state. On the basis of this error, the changed variable of the stored weight coefficient is calculated and the weight is repetitively updated. Then a learning operation is finished when the error is satisfactorily reduced.

column13: An automated response system (e.g., an automated voice response system) may employ learning strategies to develop or improve automated response capabilities. Learning strategies may include using communications(e.g., utterances, text messages, etc.) of one party in a conversation (e.g., a customer service agent) to identify and categorize communications of another party in the conversation (e.g., a caller). Classifiers can be build from the categorized communications. Classifiers can be used to identify common communications patterns of a party in a conversation (e.g., an agent). Learning strategies may also include selecting communications as learning opportunities to improve automated response capabilities based on selection criteria (e.g., selection criteria chosen to ensure that the system does not learn from unreliable or insignificant examples).

column13: PURPOSE:To reduce the learning time of a learning machine by dynamically selecting weight change direction from plural directions and changing the weight, and dynamically setting a learning parameter and an acceleration parameter where the error becomes the least. CONSTITUTION:An input signal is weighted and added at a hidden layer 22 and an output layer 22, an output signal is obtained by executing a non-linear processing, and the change direction of the weigh is decided at a weight change direction decision part to minimize the error between a desirable output signal outputted by an instruction signal generation part 13 and the output signal of the output layer. After that, the weight change amount to the plural learning parameter is obtained by a straight line location part 67, and while checking whether the learning is not in an inefficient state by an unsuitable learning state detection part, each weight is changed by adding a fixed amount times the weight change amount of the last time to the weight change amount to the learning parameter with the minimum error obtained by the straight line location part 67 at a weight change part 65. Thus, the error is made sufficiently small by repeating to the change direction of the weight. Thus, the error becomes sufficiently small in a short learning time, and the learning is ended.

column13: PURPOSE:To decrease an arithmetic quantity by providing an error signal deciding means, a weight coefficient deciding means, and a weight coefficient varying means, and setting an extremely small weight coefficient to 0 without calculating the variation quantity of the weight coefficient. CONSTITUTION:A weight coefficient update means 205 is supplied with a tutor signal as a desired value of the output signal of an output signal calculating means 1 to find the error between the output signal and tutor signal, and calculates the variation quantity of the weight coefficient corresponding to the output to decide low learning is advanced. Then when it is decided that the learning is advanced sufficiently, the weight coefficient is set to 0 if the absolute value of the weight coefficient is less than a constant threshold value, varied according to the weight coefficient variation quantity if not. Thus, an extremely small weight coefficient is set to 0 without calculating its variation quantity to reduce the arithmetic quantity.

column13: A neural network 102 receives a state signal 302 representing a state of an environment and processes the signal according to network parameter values 104 to generate an action to be performed in response to the state signal. The training of the neural network comprises using a Sequential Monte Carlo process to perform a search to determine an action associated with the current state of the environment, storing the determined action in association with the state signal for updating the network parameter values, and updating the state of the environment according to the action. The Sequential Monte Carlo process comprises generating a plurality of simulations each comprising a respective sequence of states and selected actions, each sequence starting at a first state and ending in a respective second state wherein the actions are selected using the neural network, and wherein at each state a weight value associated with the respective simulation is updated storing data indicative of the plurality of simulations, and determining the target action by evaluating the weight values associated with the plurality of simulations.

column12: Learning machine
column13: 1,011,685. Learning machines. STANDARD TELEPHONES & CABLES Ltd. July 5, 1963 [July 12, 1962], No. 26835/62. Heading G4R. An electrical learning machine comprises output means able to alter the machine's environment or its relationship with it, input means which obtains data words relating to the environment, a store with a compartment for each of the conditions of the output means, each of which can contain a data word, a comparator for comparing a received data word with those stored and for selecting the one least like the received data word, control means responsive to the selection to cause the output means to generate a corresponding output condition and means for altering the stored word to make it less unlike the input data word whereby the machine is caused to explore its environment. The machine may be applied to character readers or to control a process, e.g. a chemical process by receiving data from certain positions in the processing plant and exercising appropriate controls. As described, the machine is a movable device having six photo-cells designed to " see " different patterns in different positions and orientations. It has controls to perform movements, forward, backwards, right or left, one of which is selected at a time. The machine operates at a "search" level, a " reward " level and a " correlation " level. In the " search " level eye 1, Fig. 1, is used and memory 2, a dissimilarity detector 4 and a memory improver 3. In memory 2 there is a row for each of the four actions that can be performed by the motor control 5. Each row contains a pattern which is compared with patterns from the eye by detector 4 which selects the pattern least similar and causes the motor to perform that action. While the machine is carrying out the action, or trying to, the improver 3 causes the selected stored pattern to be made more similar to the eye pattern. As the machine moves the pattern received by the eye changes and, since the improver changes the pattern for greater similarity, the row selected by detector 4 ceases to have the least similar pattern and the detector 4 selects another row so that the action changes and the machine thereby explores its environment. In the " reward " operation a longterm step memory 6 is used together with a threshold detector 7, a sequence store 10, Fig. 2 (connected as shown in Fig. 4), and a random selector 9. Each row of memory 6 can hold an eye pattern and an action of the motor control. This is termed a " step ". The sequence store holds information in each row that the pattern and action in the corresponding row of memory 6 were followed by the pattern and action of another row, or were followed by a reward. Each row of memory 6 has a " weight " which increases with use and decreases with disuse. Memory 6 and store 10 therefore provide a record of paths which the system has previously followed and these are paths which led to a reward because steps are only stored when a reward is received. The detector 7 selects those rows of the step memory 6 having more than a certain degree of match with the eye pattern and one of them is chosen at random by selector 9 having regard to the weight of the row and the number of steps between the row and a reward. Store 10 also holds a record of those steps which led to " pain " warnings. The match threshold of detector 7 varies according to rules of which two are that the threshold rises to 1 : 1 match on receipt of each reward and thereafter decreases slowly exponentially and that the threshold is normally at a maximum and is reduced when none of the patterns in the memory 6 matches the pattern seen by the eye. In any case there is a minimum for the threshold level. The first rule causes the system to follow accurately paths which frequently lead to reward but when there has been no reward for some time it tries new paths until it discovers more rewarding paths, the threshold then becoming high again. The second rule causes the system to follow old, learned paths precisely as long as the patterns seen are the same as those stored but when they are not, the threshold is reduced and allows less complete matches to be made. In the " correlation level " of operation use is made of four correlation matrices one for each of the motor actions. A certain action may tend to change the eye pattern from pattern 1 to pattern 2. A matrix is formed by multiplying the first pattern by each term of the second. If the operation is repeated many times the matrix tends asymptotically to a form which indicates the invariant aspects of the way the corresponding action transforms pattern 1 to pattern 2. These matrices Fig. 3, can therefore be used to make a guess as to what pattern will follow a given action if the initial pattern is known. As they are used in correlation level operation they enable system to choose paths which have a higher than chance probability of leading it to a reward. Generally they enable the system to explore the consequences of future action and to take anticipatory precautions. The eye pattern received is transformed in those by the matrices and ifone transformation corresponds to a reward pattern in store 6 the corresponding action is performed. If not the system reverts to the search operation. The correlation matrices help the device if it loses its way on a remembered path, e.g. by the sequence store anticipating a certain pattern which fails to appear. The eye.-The eye consists of 6 photo-cells with suitable masking so that each looks in a different direction forwards, sideways and backwards. The photo-cells can consist of a photo transistor and a transistor output amplifier operates a slow-to-release relay. The relay is operated only when the light exceeds a predetermined value. The six relays give the eye pattern which is applied to memory 2 and store 6 in Fig. 1. Memory 2.-The short term memory 2, consists of an array of triode triggers arranged in columns and rows. A two-position relay contact applies positive voltage to one or the other tubes of the trigger, the contacts for each column being contacts of the relay of one of the eye photo-cells. The patterns stored in the rows of triggers are thereby continuously compared with the eye pattern. If the relay contact corresponds with the trigger setting positive voltage is applied to the conducting tube and a voltage drop appears across the common cathode resistor. The cathode resistors for each row are connected in parallel to the cathodes of trigger tubes forming the dissimilarity detector 4. The tube receiving the lowest signal fires and prevents the others firing. A relay in series with the conducting tube is energized and causes energization of a corresponding control coil in the motor control 5. The motor control governs the direction of the drive motor and the engagement of steering clutches. The motor also drives a random improver 3, Fig. 1, which consists of a device operating like a roulette table, a ball-bearing moving about on a rotating metal disc constrained by an insulating wall having terminals which are contacted by the ball in a random manner. There are six such terminals connected to the column conductors of matrix 2 to apply random pulses. The arrangement is such that these pulses are allowed to reach and switch the triggers only of that row which has been selected by the dissimilarity detector 4, the other rows having short-circuit paths through contacts of the relays of detector 4. While each action thus selected is being carried out the eye pattern is temporarily entered in the first empty row of store 6 and the associated action is stored in the corresponding row of matrix 8. These patterns are held until, in the next cycle the eye has seen a new pattern and the detector 7 has determined whether it has any similar patterns already in the store 6. Store 6 is similar in general principle to memory 2, triggers being connected with the eye relays so that each row gives a match signal. A " reward " button causes a capacitor to charge, the charge decaying thereafter. This signal is applied to one side of a valve-relay circuit acting as a threshold device the capacitor providing a falling threshold. Initially the level is high and only perfectly matching patterns in the store give an output. Later partial matches are accepted. The action, one of the four possibles, is stored in matrix 8 in the form of a combinational setting of two triggers. A " weight " store has a row for each row of store 6 having inputs to receive positive and negative weight pulses which are stored in capacitors each providing the bias for a triode. The currents in the triodes are determined by the weight stored in the corresponding capacitor. The anode potential of each valve is compared with that of an associated triode also controlled by a storage capacitor. These capacitors are discharged by a random selector, a relay coil between the anodes being operated when the voltage is great enough. Another relay is connected in the anode circuit of each of the first triodes which releases when the weight charge falls to zero. There is also a sequence store 10, Fig. 2, consisting of an array of relays each at the cross point of vertical and horizontal conductors. Each relay when energized establishes a connection between a pair of rows. It enables the control circuit to determine the relationship or sequence between various stored patterns. Operation.-In the " search " operation described above, supposing that no " reward ' has been received, the pattern seen by the eye is temporarily held in the first vacant row (i.e. the first) of the store 6 and the resulting action is stored in the same row of matrix 8. The patterns stored are all removed at the end of the cycle until the reward button is pressed by an operator to indicate that the device is now in a reward area. column13: A computer-implemented method (200), a machine learning system (100), and non- transitory computer-readable storage medium (400) for training a neural network (102) are provided. The neural network (102) is used to instruct an agent (106) to select actions for interacting with an environment (108) to determine a solution to a specified problem. In the computer-implemented method (200) a state signal (116) representing a current state of the environment (108) is received. A Sequential Monte Carlo process is then used to perform a search to determine target action selection data (306) associated with the current state of the environment (108). This target action selection data (306) is stored in association with the state signal (116) and the current state of the environment (108) is updated by providing an action selection signal (114) based on the target action selection data (306). The Sequential Monte Carlo process involves generating a plurality of simulations using the neural network (102) to determine the target action selection data (306).

The infrared receiving element is configured to receive infrared light emitted by the infrared emitting element and reflected by the card slot or the card; and the control element is in communication connection with the infrared emission element and the infrared receiving element and is configured to generate a correspondingly changed electric signal based on the light intensity change of the infrared light received by the infrared receiving element before and after the card is inserted into the card slot, and when the change of the electric signal reaches a preset value, the control element controls the identification element to start identifying card information. Compared with a traditional mechanical detection structure, the learning machine has no mechanical contact with the card in the detection process, so that the durability problem of the mechanical detection structure is solved, and the service life of the learning machine is greatly prolonged.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING DEVICE AND MACHING LEARNING METHOD OF MACHINE LEARNING DEVICE
column13: A machine learning method of a machine learning device including a machine learning classifier includes receiving, at the machine learning device, an image and first class information associated with the image, generating, at the machine learning device, second class information associated with the image by performing classification on the image by using the machine learning classifier, and as the second class information is generated, updating, at the machine learning device, the machine learning classifier by performing a first learning operation when a guide map is received together with the image and performing, at the machine learning device, a second learning operation different from the first learning operation when the guide map is not received together with the image.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING DEVICE AND MACHING LEARNING METHOD OF MACHINE LEARNING DEVICE
column13: The present invention relates to a training method of a machine learning apparatus including a machine learning classifier. The training method of a machine learning apparatus, which is performed by the machine learning apparatus, comprises the steps of: performing classification on an image by using the machine learning classifier to classify second class information associated with the image; and when the second class information is classified, performing a first learning operation when a guide map is received together with the image and performing a second learning operation different from the first learning operation when the guide map is not received together with the image so as to update the machine learning classifier. Therefore, machine learning can be performed without performance degradation.

column13: To provide a machine learning device, a machine learning method, a machine learning program and an inference device that can efficiently learn a statistical model of a VQA task.SOLUTION: A machine learning device according to an embodiment comprises: a conversion unit; and a learning unit. The conversion unit generates a learning sample of a VQA format associated with a VQA task on the basis of a sample of a non-VQA format. The learning sample of the VQA format has a combination of an object, a question sentence associated with the object, and a reply sentence for the question sentence as elements, and the sample of the non-VQA format has a combination of an object and a label associated with the object as elements. The learning unit trains a statistical model of the VQA task on the basis of the learning sample of the VQA format generated by the conversion unit.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 2

column13: Provided are a machine learning apparatus, a machine learning system, a machine learning method, and a storage medium, each of which has high learning accuracy while acquiring parameter data from a plurality of devices and increasing the amount of training data.The machine learning apparatus includes an acquisition unit that acquires third data including first data and second data, the first data including at least one of parameter data collected for a plurality of collection devices and training data created from the parameter data, and the second data being associated with the first data and representing collection conditions of the parameter data; a selection unit that selects specific data from the third data; and a learning unit that performs machine learning using the specific data, and generates a learned model which is to be used for a target device. column13: The invention provides a machine learning device and method, and the device comprises an objective table, the upper side of the objective table is provided with a lighting device, the upper side of the lighting device is provided with a camera, the positions of the lighting device and the camera can be adjusted in the vertical direction, the center of the lighting device is provided with a through hole, and the through hole is communicated with the camera. And the camera shoots through the through hole. The ratio value of the projection area of the shot object to the shooting distance can be obtained through learning, so that the shooting distance is adjusted according to the ratio value and the size of the shot object; the optimal shooting pattern is obtained by shooting and comparing the example pattern, so that the proper illumination intensity in the current illumination environment is determined, the equipment has the capability of automatically adjusting the shooting distance and the illumination intensity during shooting, and the technology aims at controlling the shooting distance and the shooting illumination intensity to obtain a picture with better quality, so that the shooting efficiency is improved. And the requirements of users are met.

column12: TRANSFER LEARNING WITH MACHINE LEARNING SYSTEMS
column13: Transfer learning in machine learning can include receiving a machine learning model. Target domain training data for reprogramming the machine learning model using transfer learning can be received. The target domain training data can be transformed by performing a transformation function on the target domain training data. Output labels of the machine learning model can be mapped to target labels associated with the target domain training data. The transformation function can be trained by optimizing a parameter of the transformation function. The machine learning model can be reprogrammed based on input data transformed by the transformation function and a mapping of the output labels to target labels.

column12: Machine learning architecture for lifelong learning
column13: Some embodiments described herein cover a machine learning architecture with a separated perception subsystem and application subsystem. These subsystems can be co-trained. In one example embodiment, a data item is received and information from the data item is processed by a first node to generate a sparse feature vector. A second node processes the sparse feature vector to determine an output. A relevancy rating associated with the output is determined. A determination is made as to whether to update the first node based on update criteria associated with the first node, wherein the update criteria comprise a relevancy criterion and a novelty criterion. The second node is updated based on the relevancy rating.

column12: LEARNING COACH FOR MACHINE LEARNING SYSTEM
LEARNING COACH FOR MACHINE LEARNING SYSTEM
column13: A machine learning (ML) system includes a student ML system, a learning coach ML system, and a reference system that generates training data for the student ML system. The learning coach ML system learns to make an enhancement to the student ML system or to its learning process, such as updated hyperparameter or a network structural change, based on training of the student ML system with the training data generated by the reference system. The system may also comprise a learning experimentation system that communicates with the reference system to conduct experiments on the learning of the student learning system. Also, the learning experimentation system can determine a cost function for the learning coach ML system.

column13: A machine learning system includes a coach machine learning system that uses machine learning to help a student machine learning system learn its system. By monitoring the student learning system, the coach machine learning system can learn (through machine learning techniques) “hyperparameters” for the student learning system that control the machine learning process for the student learning system. The machine learning coach could also determine structural modifications for the student learning system architecture. The learning coach can also control data flow to the student learning system.

column12: CONFIGURABLE MACHINE LEARNING SYSTEMS THROUGH GRAPHICAL USER INTERFACES
column13: Systems and methods for presenting configurable machine learning systems through graphical user interfaces are disclosed. In an embodiment, a machine learning server computer stores one or more machine learning configuration files. A particular machine learning configuration file of the one or more machine learning configuration files comprises instructions for configuring a machine learning system of a particular machine learning type with one or more first machine learning parameters. The machine learning server computer displays through a graphical user interface, a plurality of selectable parameter options, each of which defining a value for a machine learning parameter. The machine learning server computer receives a particular input dataset. The machine learning server computer additionally receives, through the graphical user interface, a selection of one or more selectable parameter options corresponding to one or more second machine learning parameters different from the one or more first machine learning parameters. The machine learning server computer replaces in the particular machine learning configuration file, the one or more first machine learning parameters with the one or more second machine learning parameters. Using the particular machine learning configuration file, the machine learning server computer configures a particular machine learning system. Using the particular machine learning system and the particular input dataset, the machine learning server computer computes a particular output dataset.

column12: Transient learning of machine learning models
column13: Embodiments disclosed herein relate to transient learning of a machine learning ("ML") model based on gradient (s) generated at a remote system (e.g., a remote server). A processor (s) of a remote system can receive, from a client device of a user, a stream of audio data (s) that captures spoken utterance (s). The fulfillment pipeline can process the audio data stream (s) such that the particular fulfillment (s) of the spoken utterance (s) is performed. At the same time, the training pipeline can process the audio data stream (s) using an unsupervised learning technique to generate the gradient (s). After processing by fulfilling the pipeline and/or training the pipeline, the audio data stream (s) are discarded by the remote system. Thus, ML model (s) can be trained at a remote system without storing or recording the audio data stream through its non-transitory memory, providing a more efficient training mechanism for training ML model (s) and increasing the security of user data.

column12: Learning coach for machine learning system
column13: A machine learning (ML) system includes a student ML system, a learning coach ML system, and a reference system that generates training data for the student ML system. The learning coach ML system learns to make an enhancement to the student ML system or to its learning process, such as updated hyperparameter or a network structural change, based on training of the student ML system with the training data generated by the reference system. The system may also comprise a learning experimentation system that communicates with the reference system to conduct experiments on the learning of the student learning system. Also, the learning experimentation system can determine a cost function for the learning coach ML system.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING DEVICE, MACHINE LEARNING METHOD, AND PROGRAM
column13: A machine learning device according to one embodiment of the present invention is provided with a storage unit and a processor. The storage unit stores answer data including: parameters presented in relation to measurement of a psychological measurement function; and answer results for the parameters from users. The processor is provided with an evaluation function application unit, a model training unit, a parameter selection unit, and a parameter presentation unit. The evaluation function application unit acquires evaluation values by applying an evaluation function to the answer results. The model training unit repeatedly feeds the parameters and the evaluation values of the answer data into a machine learning model to cause the same to learn through machine learning, thereby generating a trained model corresponding to the psychological measurement function. The parameter selection unit selects the parameters to be presented by Bayesian optimization based on the evaluation values and the trained model. The parameter presentation unit presents the selected parameters to subject users.

column12: MACHINE LEARNING MODEL UPDATE FOR MACHINE LEARNING ACCELERATOR
column13: To execute a machine learning model within a peripheral I/O device supporting both I/O and coherent domains.SOLUTION: Computational resources within a coherent domain of peripheral I/O devices communicate with a host in a manner similar to inter-CPU communication within the host. Dual domains within the peripheral I/O devices are leveraged for a machine learning (ML) application. While the I/O devices are sometimes used as ML accelerators, these accelerators previously only used an I/O domain. The computational resources are divided between the I/O domain and the coherent domain, with an ML engine in the I/O domain and an ML model in the coherent domain. The advantage is that the ML model can be coherently updated using a reference ML model stored within the host.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 4

column12: MACHINE LEARNING SYSTEM, MACHINE LEARNING METHOD, AND PROGRAM
column13: Machine learning techniques which allow machine learning to be performed even when a cost function is not a convex function are provided. A machine learning system includes a plurality of node portions which learn mapping that uses one common primal variable by machine learning based on their respective input data while sending and receiving information to and from each other. The machine learning is performed so as to minimize, instead of a cost function of a non-convex function originally corresponding to the machine learning, a proxy convex function serving as an upper bound on the cost function. The proxy convex function is represented by a formula of a first-order gradient of the cost function with respect to the primal variable or by a formula of a first-order gradient and a formula of a second-order gradient of the cost function with respect to the primal variable.

column12: Hidden machine learning for federated learning
column13: A method includes providing a distributed instance of a machine learning model to a client computing device and access to a predetermined token associated with a predetermined label to a client-side application at the client computing device. The client-side application is configured to cause the client computing device to detect a user input that includes the first predetermined token, provide the user input to the distributed instance to obtain a first predicted label for the first predetermined token, and update a set of model parameters of the distributed instance based on the first predicted label and the first predetermined label. The method may also include updating the machine learning model by obtaining the one or more updated model parameters of the distributed instance from the client computing device.

column12: Continuous learning for machine learning models
column13: A first neural network (NN) model may generate labels for training a second NN model. The second NN model may represent instances of a NN model operating on multiple different devices (e.g., decentralized user and/or edge devices). The system may include using a “teacher” model to process data received by one or more of the devices to generate a labeled dataset. The system may use the labeled dataset and a “student” model to calculate gradient data for updating the student model. The student model may be the same or similar to NN model instances operating on the devices. The system may validate the updated student model to determine, for example, whether it exhibits improved performance when processing the newly received data and/or historical data. The system may distribute the validated update to the devices.

column12: ON-THE-FLY DEEP LEARNING IN MACHINE LEARNING AT AUTONOMOUS MACHINES
column13: A mechanism is described for facilitating on-the-fly deep learning in machine learning for autonomous machines. A method of embodiments, as described herein, includes detecting an output associated with a first deep network serving as a user-independent model associated with learning of one or more neural networks at a computing device having a processor coupled to memory. The method may further include automatically generating training data for a second deep network serving as a user-dependent model, where the training data is generated based on the output. The method may further include merging the user-independent model with the user-dependent model into a single joint model.
